jokes.text: and help us to further understand that curious race we call human.
jokes.setup: Dick speaks about Tommy going to college to expand his mind
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're aliens studying humans, not humans themselves
jokes.explanation: Establishes the show's core premise with elegant efficiency - they're aliens studying humans. Good character integration and meta humor.

jokes.text: Yeah. Plus I don't have to live with you freaks anymore, you know?
jokes.setup: Emotional moment about Tommy going to college
jokes.punchline: Tommy casually calls his alien family 'freaks' breaking the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tommy's blunt honesty and deflation of sentiment. Economical delivery.

jokes.text: Hey. Ah-choo! No, not cry. Sneeze.
jokes.setup: Harry says he feels like he's gonna cry
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was actually going to sneeze, not cry emotionally
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke with good structure and economy. Alien character not understanding human emotions.

jokes.text: Who are they? Oh, we're interviewing for your replacement, so if you could just... Bye-Bye, Tommy!
jokes.setup: Tommy asks who the people are
jokes.punchline: They're already interviewing replacements while he's still there, then immediately dismiss him
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and cringe humor. The casual dismissal after Tommy's still standing there creates awkward comedy.

jokes.text: I'm hoping I can leverage my skill set to maximize the mission's effectiveness. You hear that? She's gonna somethin' the mission's whatever.
jokes.setup: Christie gives corporate speak about leveraging skills
jokes.punchline: Harry completely mangles and dismisses the corporate jargon
jokes.explanation: Great character moment for Harry showing his inability to process corporate speak. The 'somethin'' and 'whatever' construction is quotable.

jokes.text: So, uh, can we reach you at the transmitter in your head? I have a machine.
jokes.setup: Dick asks about alien communication methods
jokes.punchline: Christie matter-of-factly mentions having an answering machine instead
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of alien expectations with mundane reality. The deadpan delivery works well.

jokes.text: So, uh, Christie, how you gettin' along with your body? You like the boobs? No. No, I really don't.
jokes.setup: Dick casually asks about Christie's experience with her human body
jokes.punchline: Christie's blunt negative response about having breasts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant alien perspective on human anatomy. The casual discussion of body parts as if they're accessories is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Mmm. Yeah. The smaller ones aren't as much fun.
jokes.setup: Christie says she doesn't like having breasts
jokes.punchline: Dick's inappropriate comparison suggesting he has experience
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that plays into Dick's inappropriate nature while maintaining the alien body-switching premise.

jokes.text: So what do you think, Harry? Well, I think she's our man.
jokes.setup: Sally asks what Harry thinks about female candidate Christie
jokes.punchline: Harry calls the woman 'our man' - gender confusion
jokes.explanation: Simple gender mix-up joke that fits Harry's character as someone who doesn't quite grasp human conventions.

jokes.text: You're hired. Really? That's great. Thank you. I'm Bryce canyon.
jokes.setup: Sally hires someone instantly without asking questions
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously over-the-top name 'Bryce Canyon'
jokes.explanation: The name 'Bryce Canyon' is absurdly specific and suggests an alien trying too hard to sound human. Setup for later jokes.

jokes.text: I think you just wanna hire him because he's a pretty boy. Fine. You want a question? Ok. Bryce, when can you start? Now. That is the greatest answer to any question I have ever...
jokes.setup: Harry accuses Sally of hiring based on looks, Sally defiantly asks one question
jokes.punchline: Sally's completely over-the-top reaction to the mundane answer 'Now'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Sally's bias and over-enthusiasm. The buildup and cutoff works well for the character.

jokes.text: These walls are real cinder block. Oh, I don't think so. Dear god, they are!
jokes.setup: Tommy notes the walls are cinder block
jokes.punchline: Dick's horrified realization that human college conditions are actually that bad
jokes.explanation: Good alien perspective on normal dorm conditions. Dick's horror at typical college life is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I guess he's asleep. I don't think he's asleep. I think he's passed out. Kahlua and creme.
jokes.setup: Tommy thinks his roommate is sleeping
jokes.punchline: Dick corrects that he's passed out drunk, then identifies the specific drink
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from innocent to concerning, with Dick's specific drink identification showing alien analytical nature.

jokes.text: I know he's unconscious, but I really get that kinda buddies-for-life vibe, you know?
jokes.setup: Tommy's roommate is passed out drunk
jokes.punchline: Tommy optimistically sees this as the basis for a lifelong friendship
jokes.explanation: Tommy's naive optimism about human behavior creates good irony. His alien perspective on college friendship is endearing.

jokes.text: If you wanted to say that you learned a lot from me, too... that would be fine. Uh... I mean, I must've learned something.
jokes.setup: Dick fishing for compliments about Tommy learning from him
jokes.punchline: Tommy's reluctant, uncertain response that he 'must've' learned something
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Dick's neediness and Tommy's blunt honesty. The awkward pause is palpable.

jokes.text: 5, if you're doin' it right.
jokes.setup: Dick says he'll see Tommy in 4 years
jokes.punchline: Tommy's casual suggestion that 5 years is better for college
jokes.explanation: Simple college slacker joke delivered with good economy and deadpan timing.

jokes.text: Well, earth beings, otherwise known as humans, get around in cars, which have 4 wheels. Humans... have no wheels.
jokes.setup: Bryce is giving information about humans
jokes.punchline: The obvious and useless observation that humans don't have wheels
jokes.explanation: Good alien perspective presenting obvious human facts as revelations. Sets up Bryce as incompetent.

jokes.text: Yeah! We've known that for weeks, Bryce.
jokes.setup: Bryce gives obvious information about humans having no wheels
jokes.punchline: Harry's exasperated response that this is old news
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Bryce's incompetence. Harry as the exasperated straight man works well.

jokes.text: Well, uh... there are only 2 bagpipe factories in the world. One in edinburgh, scotland, 6 grant road, and the other's in... New guy, new guy, New guy! You've gotta be more specific! What side of the Road?
jokes.setup: Bryce gives overly specific but incomplete information about bagpipe factories
jokes.punchline: Harry's frantic demand for even more absurdly specific details
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd specificity. The contrast between useless detail and missing crucial info is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: New guy, new guy, New guy! You've gotta be more specific! What side of the Road?
jokes.setup: Bryce giving specific but useless information
jokes.punchline: Harry demanding even more specific useless details
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurd demand for specificity. Harry's intensity over bagpipe factories is hilarious.

jokes.text: Oops. I dropped my pencil. Oops. Oop... hey!
jokes.setup: Sally claims to drop her pencil around attractive Bryce
jokes.punchline: The obvious sexual harassment through repeated 'accidents'
jokes.explanation: Standard sexual harassment comedy setup. Works for character but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: Say, Bryce, we need some sunbathing information. What do you say you grease up, hit the rooftop, and I'll check on your progress later, huh?
jokes.setup: Sally asks Bryce to do research
jokes.punchline: The 'research' is clearly an excuse to see him shirtless and oiled up
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Sally's inappropriate behavior. The thin excuse of 'research' is appropriately transparent.

jokes.text: Don't you dare say that. That's Tommy's chair. That'll always be Tommy's chair. He's not dead. He's just in a different location! That's all! Just in a different location!
jokes.setup: Dick says it's not Tommy's chair anymore, it's Bryce's
jokes.punchline: Sally's increasingly hysterical defense of chair ownership
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Sally's attachment to Tommy. The repetition of 'different location' shows her trying to convince herself.

jokes.text: Ah, forget the roof. You know, there's a little light comin' in here. You just lie down right there.
jokes.setup: Bryce asks where the roof is for sunbathing research
jokes.punchline: Sally abandons all pretense and just wants him to lie down in the office
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Sally's harassment. The abandonment of even basic pretense is appropriately cringe-worthy.

jokes.text: Your dad? That's cute. See ya.
jokes.setup: Tommy introduces Dick as his dad
jokes.punchline: Cindy's immediate dismissal and departure upon hearing this
jokes.explanation: Simple comedy of Dick being a cockblock. The immediate exit is well-timed and economical.

jokes.text: See ya later, cind. See ya later, cind. I think she thinks you're a dork.
jokes.setup: Dick tries to be cool by shortening Cindy's name
jokes.punchline: Tommy's blunt assessment that she thinks Dick is a dork
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Tommy's blunt honesty deflating Dick's attempt to be cool.

jokes.text: Oh, no, but it's gotten much better. We just painted.
jokes.setup: Dick admits Pendleton sucks but says it's gotten better
jokes.punchline: The only improvement is that they painted
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery of a pathetic improvement. The minimal nature of the change is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Yeah. You know, that sort of anti-social, rebellious act that wreaks havoc, but makes you the most popular man on campus.
jokes.setup: Tommy asks about freshman pranks
jokes.punchline: Dick's contradiction that anti-social behavior makes you popular
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the prank storyline. Dick's alien misunderstanding of human social dynamics.

jokes.text: Something much more destructive than that. You've got to do some real damage if you want to set the girls' hearts a-flutter.
jokes.setup: Tommy suggests a goldfish in the water cooler as a prank
jokes.punchline: Dick's escalation that real damage will attract women
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and setup for Tommy being manipulated into vandalism. Dick's twisted advice fits his character.

jokes.text: Well, sir, you could try yellow, or maybe dijon... you're just guessing. I know. I'm sorry. I'm a screw-up.
jokes.setup: Bryce gives mustard suggestions
jokes.punchline: Harry calls him out for guessing and Bryce immediately breaks down
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bryce's incompetence and low self-esteem. The immediate self-deprecation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: About that... it feels so tight, especially under the arms. Hmm. You know what's good for that? Vaseline.
jokes.setup: Harry says Bryce should get comfortable in his own skin
jokes.punchline: Bryce takes it literally, complaining about his skin being tight
jokes.explanation: Excellent alien literal interpretation of a human idiom. The vaseline suggestion is perfectly absurd and Harry's matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Gum can double as adhesive, But most adhesives do not necessarily double as gum.
jokes.setup: Harry giving advice to Bryce
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd and useless advice about gum and adhesive
jokes.explanation: Brilliant nonsensical advice delivered with complete seriousness. The logic is perfectly illogical and very quotable.

jokes.text: No. No. You don't have to call me 'Sir.' Call me 'Your honor.' Your honor. Yeah. Little bit louder. Your honor.
jokes.setup: Harry tells Bryce not to call him sir
jokes.punchline: Harry wants an even more grandiose title and coaching on delivery
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Harry's ego and need for respect. The coaching on volume is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. He'll have to flip. He'll have to flip! Ha HA!
jokes.setup: They've turned the office upside down as a prank
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation of 'flip out' as physically flipping
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay based on the visual prank. The escalating laughter sells the alien amusement at their own joke.

jokes.text: Dick, open the door. You have the... You have the doorknob. I know. Oh, my god! You planned this. You're trying to get me expelled! Bingo!
jokes.setup: They're trapped in the room they vandalized
jokes.punchline: Dick's cheerful admission that this was all planned
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal and escalation. Dick's manipulation is revealed perfectly, and 'Bingo!' is a great character moment showing his satisfaction.

jokes.text: And that's why old men wear their pants higher than young men. That is fantastically useful.
jokes.setup: Harry has been giving Bryce information all night
jokes.punchline: More completely useless 'wisdom' that Bryce finds valuable
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Harry's pattern of useless advice. Bryce's enthusiasm for nonsense maintains character consistency.

jokes.text: Well, that's my job. Ah, you know, you're making quite a name for yourself in the organization. 'Sally's boy wonder,' that's what they're calling you. Who's calling me that? Sally.
jokes.setup: Sally says Bryce is being called 'Sally's boy wonder'
jokes.punchline: Only Sally herself is calling him that
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff showing Sally's self-serving manipulation. The economy and structure are excellent.

jokes.text: How can 'All the way' Be just the beginning? I like the way you think, Bryce... but you can stop now.
jokes.setup: Sally says Bryce can go 'all the way' but that's just the beginning
jokes.punchline: Bryce's logical confusion and Sally's dismissal when he questions it
jokes.explanation: Good logical absurdity. Bryce's literal alien thinking clashes with Sally's seductive nonsense.

jokes.text: Well, Uh, uh, traffic lights. Green means 'Go.' Aah! You cracked the code.
jokes.setup: Sally wants more research from Bryce
jokes.punchline: He gives the most obvious traffic light information and she sarcastically celebrates
jokes.explanation: Callback to Bryce's pattern of stating the obvious. Sally's sarcasm works well with the setup.

jokes.text: I'm beginning to wonder if you care about traffic lights at all! Oh, Bryce, no!
jokes.setup: Bryce tries to leave Sally's seduction attempt
jokes.punchline: Sally's dramatic accusation about not caring about traffic lights
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the sexual harassment plot through completely absurd accusation about traffic lights. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: Sure. Blame dad. I suppose you'll be sending this good-for-nothing home with me. Well, it is a serious offense. Where are the expulsion forms, in your desk? I could give you a leg up.
jokes.setup: Chancellor considers Tommy's punishment
jokes.punchline: Dick eagerly offering to help with expulsion paperwork
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Dick's eagerness to get Tommy kicked out. The helpful offer to assist with expulsion is hilariously inappropriate.

jokes.text: Tommy, for your infraction, I am issuing a warning. That's it, Tommy. Pack your bags. Get in the car... A warning?
jokes.setup: Chancellor announces Tommy's punishment
jokes.punchline: Dick's immediate assumption it's expulsion, then shock at the light punishment
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character comedy. Dick's eagerness backfires perfectly when the punishment is minimal.

jokes.text: But a warning? Why don't you just give him a big wet kiss?
jokes.setup: Dick is outraged by the light punishment
jokes.punchline: Suggests a kiss as equivalent to a warning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dick's outrage. The absurd equivalency of warning to kiss is appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: You should wipe scum like this off your campus! I remember my own freshman prank. I messed with the marching band's formation and made 'em spell out a dirty word.
jokes.setup: Dick calls Tommy scum
jokes.punchline: Chancellor fondly remembers doing the same type of prank
jokes.explanation: Good irony with the Chancellor relating to the behavior Dick condemns. Sets up the romantic subplot nicely.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I'm gonna call her up. She ought to see this. Such attention to detail. I love the coffee mug. Thank you. The coffee mug was my idea.
jokes.setup: Chancellor wants his daughter to meet Tommy
jokes.punchline: Dick desperately claiming credit for the coffee mug detail
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dick's desperation for credit and attention. His petty claim about the coffee mug is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I mean, a Pat on your muscular back is not harassment. Well, I looked it up, and according to state laws, harassment is any verbal or physical overture that makes me feel uncomfortable, and I'm very uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Sally defends her behavior as innocent encouragement
jokes.punchline: Bryce's legalistic response citing actual harassment law
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the harassment plot. Sally's mention of 'muscular back' while claiming innocence is perfectly contradictory.

jokes.text: Well, if you're not comfortable, you should take off your pants. Casual thursday.
jokes.setup: Bryce says he's uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Sally's completely inappropriate suggestion disguised as casual workplace policy
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Sally's inappropriate behavior. The 'Casual Thursday' excuse is perfectly absurd and economical.

jokes.text: I treated you like a sexy, hunky object instead of a sexy, hunky employee, and I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Sally apologizes for her behavior
jokes.punchline: Her 'apology' still objectifies him in the exact same way
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of an apology. The structure is perfect - she seems to correct herself but actually repeats the same behavior. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Cool. In you go, you piece of meat!
jokes.setup: Sally claims to have learned her lesson about objectification
jokes.punchline: Immediately objectifies Bryce again the moment he agrees
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and escalation. Sally's complete inability to change despite claiming to learn is executed perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh, ha ha ha. You know, I don't even know who you are anymore. You've changed. Oh, yes, with your new life of learning, your internet, your nintendo, your fancy friends. Your tv, your virtual reality, your... your... and your... I... I don't even know!
jokes.setup: Dick accuses Tommy of changing
jokes.punchline: Dick's increasingly incoherent list of modern technology, ending in complete breakdown
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing Dick's fear of losing Tommy. The breakdown in speech patterns as he gets emotional is well-executed.

jokes.text: I don't want you to go. I don't want you to go.
jokes.setup: Dick's angry rant about Tommy changing
jokes.punchline: The raw emotional admission after all the posturing
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional payoff. The repetition and vulnerability after Dick's bluster creates genuine character moment within the comedy.

jokes.text: Parenting's hard. You give love, you give encouragement, you give support... and then, if you do a good job, they just go away.
jokes.setup: Dick explains the difficulty of parenting
jokes.punchline: The ironic observation that successful parenting leads to separation
jokes.explanation: Genuinely insightful observation delivered through alien character. The irony is both funny and touching, well-earned emotional moment.

jokes.text: Parental love at It's best. It's the only love there is that inevitably leads to separation. How do you always know these things? I'm the information officer.
jokes.setup: Tommy gives profound insight about parental love
jokes.punchline: Attributes his wisdom to his job title
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to Tommy's role while maintaining the emotional moment. The deadpan delivery works well with the wisdom.

jokes.text: It's the only love there is that inevitably leads to separation.
jokes.setup: Discussion about parenting
jokes.punchline: Tommy's wise observation about parental love
jokes.explanation: Beautiful, concise wisdom about the nature of parental love. Perfect economy and emotional resonance.

jokes.text: You came on to him, huh? Yeah, like Germany came on to Poland.
jokes.setup: Dick asks if Sally came on to Bryce
jokes.punchline: Sally compares her harassment to Nazi invasion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy comparison. The historical reference is unexpected and perfectly economical. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: He's separating his whites. Yeah, and maybe starting to presoak. Mmm. I hope he goes easy on the bleach, but you know Tommy. He won't. No. Never.
jokes.setup: Wondering what Tommy is doing at college
jokes.punchline: Obsessing over his laundry habits in detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending showing their intimate knowledge of Tommy's habits. The mundane detail about bleach is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: I wonder what Tommy's doing right now? He's separating his whites. Yeah, and maybe starting to presoak. Mmm. I hope he goes easy on the bleach, but you know Tommy. He won't. No. Never.
jokes.setup: They wonder what Tommy is doing at college
jokes.punchline: They know his exact laundry routine and habits in detail
jokes.explanation: Sweet ending showing how well they know Tommy despite the distance. The specific detail about bleach usage is a nice character touch.
